flags.message: This looks like a #363-class case (historical SCOTUS docket, published opinion linked, outcome only in opinion text) that escaped the is_published_opinion_unresolvable exclusion: the docket snapshot carries a cluster link, but the corpus row's citations/citation_count/opinion_text are all empty, so the predicate cannot see the opinion. The empty docket number also defeats the #309 and #333 predicates. A cluster-detail backfill (or extending the #363 signal to the docket's cluster link) would take this class out of predict scope at the source.

Snapshot is metadata-only: empty docket number, no docket entries, no parties/attorneys, every activity date null. The prediction rests on the linked-opinion-cluster signal plus corpus base rates (resolved SCOTUS events are 78% 'other'), not on any petition-stage facts.
